qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via EDITOR environment variable in config edit command
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes HTTP handlers in pdp/handlers.go, market/mk12/mk12_utils.go, and market/mk20/http/http.go passing raw database error messages to HTTP clients (CWE-209/CWE-532). This finding describes command injection via the EDITOR environment variable in cmd/curio/config.go — a completely different root cause, vulnerable code, files, and vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing authentication on critical sector file management endpoints allows unauthorized data access and deletion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes err.Error() leaking database credentials through HTTP error responses in pdp/handlers.go and market handlers (CWE-209/CWE-532). This finding describes missing authentication on /remote/* endpoints in lib/paths/http_handler.go — different handlers, different files, different root cause (missing auth guard vs verbose error messages), different vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-side request forgery via user-controlled notify URL in PDP piece upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding touches the PDP component (pdp/handlers_upload.go), the advisory describes pdp/handlers.go passing err.Error() to HTTP responses to leak database credentials (CWE-209/CWE-532). This finding's root cause is unvalidated notify URLs stored in the database and later used for HTTP POST requests in tasks/pdp/notify_task.go (CWE-918/SSRF). Different files (pdp/handlers_upload.go vs pdp/handlers.go), different vulnerable operations (URL validation vs error message sanitization), different vulnerability class (SSRF vs information exposure through error messages).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-gj6x-q8rh-wj6x) describes Curio HTTP handlers in pdp/handlers.go, market/mk12/mk12_utils.go, and market/mk20/http/http.go passing raw database error messages containing connection strings with plaintext passwords to HTTP clients via http.Error() (CWE-209/CWE-532). None of the three qualifying findings identify this root cause. Finding 1 is command injection via EDITOR env var in cmd/curio/config.go. Finding 2 is missing authentication on /remote/* endpoints in lib/paths/http_handler.go. Finding 3 is SSRF via unvalidated notify URL in pdp/handlers_upload.go and tasks/pdp/notify_task.go. All three describe different vulnerable code, different root causes, and different vulnerability classes than the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: pdp/handlers.go passes err.Error() to http.Error() leaking DB connection strings with passwords
Advisory affected files: pdp/handlers.go, market/mk12/mk12_utils.go, market/mk20/http/http.go
Advisory CWE: CWE-209 (Information Exposure Through Error Messages), CWE-532 (Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File)
vuln_command_injection_editor: cmd/curio/config.go exec.Command(editor) — CWE-77 command injection, unrelated files and root cause
vuln_missing_auth_remote_endpoints: lib/paths/http_handler.go missing auth guards — CWE-306 missing authentication, unrelated files and root cause
vuln_ssrf_notify_url: pdp/handlers_upload.go unvalidated notify URL — CWE-918 SSRF, different PDP file and different root cause than pdp/handlers.go error message leaking
